items.title: Finalize ARM64 Buildroot kernel configuration
items.description: Complete the Buildroot defconfig with all required packages for minimal bootable system including networking, filesystem, init system, cgroups, namespaces, and overlayfs for container support.
acceptance_criteria: Buildroot config produces bootable ARM64 image
Networking stack functional (bridge, veth, iptables for containers)
Init system (OpenRC) launches containerd on boot
cgroups v2 and namespaces enabled for container isolation
overlayfs enabled for container storage driver
Root filesystem type defined (ext4 or squashfs)
Boot loader configured (U-Boot for SBC targets)

items.title: Set up containerd runtime with boot integration
items.description: Install and configure containerd as the container runtime on the ARM64 base system. Ensure it starts on boot via OpenRC and Docker CLI can interact with it.
acceptance_criteria: Containerd installed and starts on boot via OpenRC service
Docker CLI can interact with containerd (pull, run, stop)
Container networking functional (bridge mode)
Containerd survives restart without data loss
Storage driver uses overlayfs on ext4 partition

items.title: Configure GPU/NPU device passthrough for containers
items.description: Enable hardware acceleration passthrough into containers. Target Tier 1 hardware: Jetson Orin (CUDA), RK3588 (RKNN), Pi 5 + Hailo-10H. Include CPU-only fallback for unsupported hardware.
acceptance_criteria: NPU/GPU devices visible inside containers via device passthrough
Jetson Orin: CUDA GPU accessible, Ollama uses GPU acceleration
RK3588: RKNN NPU accessible via /dev/rknpu, RKLLM toolkit functional
Pi 5 + Hailo-10H: Hailo device accessible via HailoRT
CPU-only fallback when no accelerator present (llama.cpp NEON/SVE)
Performance benchmark per target: tok/s for Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4

items.title: Deploy Ollama + ChromaDB AI stack via Docker Compose
items.description: Validate and harden the Docker Compose configuration for Ollama LLM server and ChromaDB vector database. Resolve LanceDB/ChromaDB mismatch in bmt_ai_os-build/. Ensure data persistence and service resilience.
acceptance_criteria: Ollama serves model inference on port 11434
ChromaDB accepts vector operations on port 8000
Services auto-restart on failure (restart policy configured)
Model data persisted via named volume (survives container restart)
ChromaDB data persisted via named volume (survives container restart)
Resource limits set (CPU, memory) appropriate per device tier
bmt_ai_os-build/services/ai-stack.yml updated to ChromaDB (remove LanceDB reference)

items.title: Implement BMT AI OS controller for AI stack orchestration
items.description: Extend the controller prototype to manage the lifecycle of AI stack containers (Ollama, ChromaDB, and future AI services). Scope is limited to AI-stack services, not system-wide container management.
acceptance_criteria: Controller starts/stops AI stack containers (Ollama, ChromaDB) on demand
Health check polling with configurable interval (default 30s)
Auto-restart failed AI stack containers
Controller exposes status endpoint or CLI showing service health
Scope limited to AI-stack services defined in docker-compose.yml
Graceful shutdown handling (SIGTERM)

items.title: Build document ingestion pipeline into ChromaDB
items.description: Create an ingestion pipeline that reads documents, chunks them, generates embeddings via Qwen3-Embedding, and stores them in ChromaDB for retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: Supports ingestion of plain text (.txt), Markdown (.md), and source code files
Documents chunked with configurable chunk size (default 512 tokens)
Embeddings generated via Ollama embedding model (default: Qwen3-Embedding or nomic-embed-text)
Chunks stored in ChromaDB with source metadata (filename, position, language)
Ingestion of 100 documents completes within 60 seconds on Jetson Orin

items.title: Build RAG query and augmented generation pipeline
items.description: Implement the retrieval-augmented generation flow: accept a user query, retrieve relevant chunks from ChromaDB, and pass context to Ollama for augmented response generation.
acceptance_criteria: User query retrieves top-k relevant chunks from ChromaDB (k configurable, default 5)
Retrieved context injected into prompt template
LLM generates augmented response using retrieved context
End-to-end query latency under 3 seconds on Jetson Orin with Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4
Response includes source attribution (which documents were used)

items.title: Design and implement LLM provider abstraction layer
items.description: Create a unified provider interface in the controller that abstracts LLM backends behind a common API. Support provider registration, configuration, and runtime switching. Local providers are primary; cloud providers are optional fallbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Provider interface defines chat(), embed(), and list_models() methods
Provider registry allows registering and selecting backends by name
Configuration file (YAML) specifies active provider and fallback chain
Ollama provider implemented as reference (including Anthropic-compatible API for Claude Code)
Provider can be switched at runtime without restarting the controller

items.title: Implement local LLM provider: vLLM
items.description: Add vLLM as a local inference backend. Higher throughput than Ollama for batch workloads. OpenAI-compatible API out of the box.
acceptance_criteria: vLLM service added to docker-compose.yml with ARM64-compatible image
Provider implements chat() and list_models() via vLLM OpenAI-compatible API
Provider implements embed() or falls back to Ollama for embeddings
Model loading and switching works via provider config
Health check integration with controller

items.title: Implement local LLM provider: llama.cpp server
items.description: Add llama.cpp (via llama-server) as a lightweight local inference backend. Best option for resource-constrained ARM64 devices. Native NEON/SVE and KleidiAI optimizations for ARM.
acceptance_criteria: llama-server service added to docker-compose.yml (or binary install)
Provider implements chat() and embed() via llama.cpp HTTP API
GGUF model format support (Q4_K_M default quantization)
Memory footprint documented vs Ollama on same model
ARM NEON/SVE acceleration verified, RKNN delegation when available

items.title: Implement cloud LLM provider: OpenAI
items.description: Add OpenAI as a cloud provider backend. Also serves as base class for OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, Mistral).
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements chat() via OpenAI Chat Completions API
Provider implements embed() via OpenAI Embeddings API
API key configured via environment variable or secrets file (never hardcoded)
Graceful error handling when offline or rate-limited
Request/response logging for cost tracking

items.title: Implement cloud LLM provider: Anthropic (Claude)
items.description: Add Anthropic Claude as a cloud provider backend. Supports tool use and extended thinking.
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements chat() via Anthropic Messages API
Handles Anthropic-specific features (system prompt, tool use)
API key configured via environment variable or secrets file
Graceful error handling when offline or rate-limited
Request/response logging for cost tracking

items.title: Implement cloud LLM provider: Google Gemini
items.description: Add Google Gemini as a cloud provider backend.
acceptance_criteria: Provider implements chat() via Gemini API
Provider implements embed() via Gemini Embedding API
API key configured via environment variable or secrets file
Graceful error handling when offline or rate-limited
Request/response logging for cost tracking

items.title: Implement cloud LLM providers: Mistral and Groq
items.description: Add Mistral and Groq as cloud provider backends. Both use OpenAI-compatible APIs, reuse OpenAI base class.
acceptance_criteria: Mistral provider implements chat() and embed() via Mistral API
Groq provider implements chat() via Groq API (OpenAI-compatible)
Shared OpenAI-compatible base class reused for both
API keys configured independently per provider
Groq noted as low-latency cloud fallback option

items.title: Implement provider fallback chain and routing logic
items.description: Build the routing layer that tries providers in priority order (local-first, cloud fallback). Handle failures gracefully with configurable retry and timeout per provider.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback chain defined in config (e.g., ollama → llama.cpp → openai → anthropic)
Automatic failover to next provider on timeout or error
Configurable timeout per provider (default 30s local, 15s cloud)
Circuit breaker: skip unhealthy providers for configurable cooldown period
Logs which provider served each request

items.title: Package and pre-install AI coding CLIs (Claude Code, Aider, Continue, Tabby)
items.description: Bundle the top AI coding CLI tools into the BMT AI OS image. Includes Tabby as self-hosted Copilot alternative (32k GitHub stars, Rust-based, supports fine-tuning on private repos).
acceptance_criteria: Claude Code installed and functional (uses Ollama Anthropic-compatible API since v0.14)
Aider installed and functional (pip package)
Continue.dev installed as CLI/server component
Tabby server installed (self-hosted code completion)
Open Interpreter installed and functional
All CLIs launch without errors on ARM64
Version pinning for reproducible installs

items.title: Auto-configure coding CLIs to use local LLM providers
items.description: Generate default configuration for each coding CLI pointing to local providers. Use Ollama Anthropic-compatible API for Claude Code, OpenAI-compatible API for Aider/Continue/Tabby.
acceptance_criteria: Claude Code configured via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
Aider defaults to local Ollama model (Qwen2.5-Coder-7B or Qwen3.5-9B)
Continue config.json auto-generated with Ollama provider (Qwen3 30B-A3B recommended)
Tabby configured with local model backend
Open Interpreter configured to use local LLM endpoint
Cloud API keys auto-detected from env vars when present
Config regenerated when provider settings change

items.title: Add IDE AI plugin support (Cursor, Copilot, Cody)
items.description: Expose OpenAI-compatible API endpoint so remote IDEs can use BMT AI OS as their AI backend. Streaming SSE support for real-time completions.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI-compatible API served on configurable port (default 8080)
Cursor can connect using custom API base URL pointing to BMT AI OS
Copilot-compatible endpoint for code completions
Cody compatible endpoint for chat and embeddings
API supports streaming responses (SSE) for real-time completions
Connection guide documented for each IDE

items.title: Add code agent support (SWE-agent, Codex CLI, Mentat)
items.description: Package autonomous code agents that can operate on local repositories using the local LLM stack. Sandboxed execution in containers with mounted workspaces.
acceptance_criteria: SWE-agent installed and runnable against local repos
Codex CLI installed with --oss flag for local Ollama backend
Mentat installed with local provider configuration
Agents can access local filesystem and git repos
Sandboxed execution: agents run in containers with mounted workspaces
Agent output logged for review

items.title: Implement workspace and project context management for coding tools
items.description: Shared workspace mounting and codebase indexing into ChromaDB so all coding CLIs and agents leverage RAG-enhanced code understanding. Use Qwen3-Embedding for code-optimized embeddings.
acceptance_criteria: Project directories mountable into tool containers via controller CLI
Codebase auto-indexed into ChromaDB on mount (using BMTOS-5a ingestion)
Indexed context available to all coding CLIs via RAG pipeline
File watcher re-indexes changed files incrementally
Multiple workspaces supported simultaneously
Workspace context isolated per project in ChromaDB collections

items.title: Create coding model preset manager
items.description: Manage recommended model presets optimized for coding tasks. Auto-detect device RAM/NPU and recommend optimal preset. Ship Qwen-family models as defaults (best open-source coding models in 2026).
acceptance_criteria: Preset 'lite': Qwen3.5-9B Q4 (~6GB RAM) for Pi 5 / RK3588 8GB
Preset 'standard': Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 + Qwen3-Embedding-8B (~8GB) for RK3588 16GB / Jetson
Preset 'full': Qwen3.5-27B Q4 + Qwen3-Embedding-8B (~18GB) for Jetson / RK3588 32GB
Auto-detect device RAM/NPU and recommend preset
One-command model pull: bmt_ai_os models install coding-standard
Preset config shared across all coding CLIs

items.title: Build bootable ARM64 image pipeline
items.description: Create the end-to-end build pipeline that takes Buildroot config + BitBake layers from bmt_ai_os-build/ and produces a flashable ARM64 image. Support multiple board targets.
acceptance_criteria: Build script produces bootable .img file for at least one Tier 1 target
Integrates bmt_ai_os-build/ BitBake layers (hardware-accel.bb, container-engine.bb)
Image boots on QEMU ARM64 emulator (qemu-system-aarch64)
Image boots on target hardware (Jetson Orin or RK3588)
Build reproducible with pinned package versions
Build artifacts documented (sizes, partitions, bootloader)

items.title: Configure init system and service boot ordering
items.description: Define the full boot sequence: network → storage → containerd → AI stack → controller. Handle first-boot initialization (volume creation, initial model pull). Use OpenRC as init system per base-config.toml.
acceptance_criteria: OpenRC service files for containerd, ollama, chromadb, controller
Service dependencies enforced (network before containerd, containerd before AI stack)
First-boot script initializes volumes, pulls default model preset
Boot-to-ready time measured and documented per target
System recovers gracefully from unclean shutdown
15+ consecutive boot cycles without failure

items.title: Set up QEMU testing and CI pipeline
items.description: Automated testing pipeline that builds the image, boots it in QEMU ARM64, and runs integration tests verifying all services come up. Essential for development without physical hardware.
acceptance_criteria: QEMU ARM64 setup scripted and documented
CI pipeline builds image and boots in QEMU on every commit
Integration tests verify: containerd running, Ollama responding, ChromaDB responding
Controller health endpoint returns healthy status
Test results logged and accessible
GitHub Actions workflow (or equivalent) committed

items.title: Design filesystem layout and storage partitioning
items.description: Define the root filesystem layout, data partition for models/vectors, and persistent storage strategy. Models (5-20GB) and vector DB data need dedicated persistent storage that survives OS updates.
acceptance_criteria: Root filesystem layout documented (/etc, /var, /opt/bmt_ai_os)
Dedicated data partition for AI models and ChromaDB volumes
Partition scheme supports A/B root for future OTA updates
Model storage location standardized (/var/lib/ollama/models)
ChromaDB data at /var/lib/chromadb with backup strategy
Disk usage documented per preset (lite ~10GB, standard ~15GB, full ~25GB)

items.title: Implement service discovery and container networking
items.description: Configure internal DNS and networking so containers resolve each other by hostname. External services accessible via host ports. Isolated container network for AI stack.
acceptance_criteria: Containers resolve each other by service name (ollama, chromadb)
Docker Compose network isolates AI stack from host network
Host can reach services via localhost ports (11434, 8000)
DNS resolution works inside containers for external lookups
Network survives container restarts without stale entries

items.title: Configure container security and secrets management
items.description: Harden container security: non-root execution, read-only filesystems where possible, capability dropping. Implement secrets management for cloud API keys.
acceptance_criteria: AI stack containers run as non-root user where possible
Secrets stored in /etc/bmt_ai_os/secrets/ with restricted permissions (0600)
Cloud API keys loaded from secrets files, never from environment in logs
Container capabilities dropped to minimum required set
No hardcoded credentials in any image layer
Security policy documented

items.title: Build BMT AI OS CLI tool (bmt_ai_os command)
items._epic_note: Standalone — cross-cutting CLI used by all epics
items.description: Create the primary user-facing CLI for interacting with BMT AI OS: manage models, query RAG, check status, configure providers. Single entry point for all OS AI capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: bmt_ai_os status — show system health, active provider, loaded model
bmt_ai_os models list/install/remove — model management via Ollama
bmt_ai_os query 'question' — RAG query against indexed workspace
bmt_ai_os ingest /path/to/docs — index documents into ChromaDB
bmt_ai_os provider set ollama|vllm|openai — switch active provider
bmt_ai_os workspace add /path — mount and index a project
Help text and --help for all subcommands

items.title: Expose external REST API for AI stack access
items.description: HTTP REST API for external apps to query the RAG pipeline, manage models, and interact with the AI stack. OpenAI-compatible endpoint for drop-in integration with any tool.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/chat — chat completion (OpenAI-compatible format)
POST /api/v1/embeddings — generate embeddings
POST /api/v1/query — RAG-augmented query with source attribution
GET /api/v1/models — list available models
GET /api/v1/health — system health check
Streaming responses via SSE for chat completions
API key authentication (optional, configurable)

items.title: Build centralized logging and system metrics
items.description: Aggregate logs from all services (controller, Ollama, ChromaDB, containers). Collect system metrics (CPU, RAM, temperature, inference tok/s). Essential for debugging and performance tuning on edge hardware.
acceptance_criteria: All container logs aggregated to syslog or journal
System metrics collected: CPU, RAM, disk, temperature
Inference metrics: tokens/sec, model load time, queue depth
bmt_ai_os logs — tail aggregated logs
bmt_ai_os metrics — show current system stats
Log retention policy: rotate at 100MB, keep 7 days

items.title: Implement OS update mechanism with rollback
items.description: Update the OS kernel, system packages, and container images with rollback capability. A/B partition scheme for safe OS updates. Container image updates independent of OS updates.
acceptance_criteria: OS update fetches new rootfs and writes to inactive partition
Bootloader switches to new partition on next boot
Automatic rollback if new partition fails health check
Container image updates independent (docker compose pull)
Model data and ChromaDB data preserved across updates
bmt_ai_os update — single command for full system update

items.title: Add Jetson Orin board support package
items.description: Specific BSP for NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super: CUDA toolkit, TensorRT-LLM, JetPack components integration. Primary Tier 1 target — 67 TOPS, 7B models at 15-22 tok/s.
acceptance_criteria: Jetson Orin Nano Super boots BMT AI OS image
CUDA GPU accessible inside containers
Ollama uses CUDA acceleration for inference
Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 achieves 15+ tok/s
TensorRT-LLM available as optional acceleration path
Thermal management: throttle inference on overtemp

items.title: Add Rockchip RK3588 board support package
items.description: BSP for RK3588 boards (Orange Pi 5, ROCK 5B): RKNN NPU driver, RKLLM toolkit integration. Best value Tier 1 target ($100-180) but worst software fragmentation.
acceptance_criteria: Orange Pi 5 or ROCK 5B boots BMT AI OS image
RKNN NPU accessible (/dev/rknpu) inside containers
RKLLM toolkit runs TinyLlama 1.1B at 15+ tok/s on NPU
CPU-only path runs Qwen3.5-9B Q4 via llama.cpp (NEON)
Board-specific device tree and U-Boot config
8GB and 16GB RAM variants tested

items.title: Add Raspberry Pi 5 + Hailo AI HAT+ 2 board support package
items.description: BSP for Pi 5 with Hailo-10H accelerator (40 TOPS, 8GB onboard RAM). Supports models up to 1.5B params at ~9.5 tok/s. Largest community of any ARM64 SBC.
acceptance_criteria: Pi 5 + AI HAT+ 2 boots BMT AI OS image
Hailo-10H device accessible via HailoRT inside containers
Qwen2.5-1.5B runs at ~9 tok/s on Hailo NPU
CPU-only fallback runs Qwen3.5-9B Q4 at ~4-6 tok/s
Power consumption documented (target <15W during inference)
8GB Pi 5 variant tested

items.title: Scaffold Next.js dashboard app with shadcn/ui
items.description: Initialize the dashboard as a Next.js App Router project with shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript. Set up project structure, theme provider (dark default), layout shell with sidebar navigation, and API route handlers that proxy to the controller backend.
acceptance_criteria: Next.js 15+ app created at bmt_ai_os/dashboard/ with App Router
shadcn/ui initialized with 'new-york' style, dark theme default
Tailwind CSS configured with shadcn/ui CSS variables
Layout shell: sidebar nav (shadcn Sidebar), top bar, main content area
Nav items: Overview, Models, RAG Console, Providers, Logs, Coding Tools
Theme toggle (dark/light) using shadcn/ui ThemeProvider (next-themes)
Dashboard served on port 9090 (standard admin/monitoring port)
API route handlers at /api/ proxying to controller backend
WebSocket hook (useWebSocket) for real-time data streaming
Docker build stage: static export or standalone for ARM64 deployment
Production build under 2MB gzipped

items.title: Build system overview dashboard page
items.description: Main dashboard landing page showing system health at a glance. Uses shadcn/ui Card, Badge, Progress components for service status, system gauges, and inference metrics. Real-time updates via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: Service health Cards: Badge (green/yellow/red) for Ollama, ChromaDB, Controller
System gauges: Progress bars for CPU %, RAM %, disk %, temperature
Active model Card with size, quantization, and provider info
Inference metrics: tok/s sparkline (recharts), avg latency, total requests
Uptime counter per service
Real-time updates via WebSocket (no polling)
Responsive grid layout: mobile (1 col) and desktop (2-3 col)
Uses shadcn/ui: Card, Badge, Progress, Separator, Skeleton (loading states)

items.title: Build model manager dashboard page
items.description: Web UI for browsing, downloading, switching, and removing LLM models. Uses shadcn/ui Table, Dialog, Button, Progress for model library, install actions, and disk usage. Streaming download progress via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: Installed models Table: name, size, quantization, last used, disk usage
Available models: Command palette (shadcn Command) to search Ollama library
One-click download with Progress bar (streaming via WebSocket)
Delete model via AlertDialog confirmation
Switch active model via Select dropdown, no page reload
Preset quick-install Buttons: lite, standard, full
Disk usage summary Card: total storage used vs available
Uses shadcn/ui: Table, Command, Dialog, AlertDialog, Button, Progress, Select

items.title: Build RAG console dashboard page
items.description: Web interface for document ingestion and RAG queries. Uses shadcn/ui for chat-style query interface with streaming responses, file upload dropzone, and collapsible source attribution panels.
acceptance_criteria: Document upload: drag-and-drop zone using Input file + custom dropzone
Workspace browser: Tabs per collection, Table of indexed docs with count
Chat-style query interface: Input + Button, streaming response via SSE
Source attribution: Collapsible panels showing matched chunks per response
Chunk preview: Accordion with highlighted matching text
Ingestion progress: Toast notifications for chunking/embedding/storage status
Collection management: create (Dialog), delete (AlertDialog), re-index (Button)
Uses shadcn/ui: Tabs, Table, Input, Button, Accordion, Collapsible, Toast, Dialog

items.title: Build provider configuration dashboard page
items.description: Web UI for configuring LLM providers. Uses shadcn/ui Switch toggles, sortable fallback chain with drag-and-drop, masked Input fields for API keys, and per-provider health Badges.
acceptance_criteria: Provider list: Cards with Switch toggle (enable/disable) per provider
Fallback chain: sortable list (dnd-kit) for drag-and-drop reordering
API key management: masked Input fields with eye toggle, save to secrets
Per-provider stats Card: requests served, avg latency, error rate
Health Badge per provider (green/yellow/red)
Test connection Button with loading Spinner and Toast result
Changes apply without controller restart (optimistic UI update)
Uses shadcn/ui: Card, Switch, Input, Button, Badge, Toast, Separator

items.title: Build logs viewer dashboard page
items.description: Real-time log viewer using shadcn/ui. Virtualized log list for performance, filter toggles per service, log level badges, and monospace ScrollArea. WebSocket streaming for live-tail.
acceptance_criteria: Live-tail logs via WebSocket in virtualized ScrollArea (react-virtual)
Filter ToggleGroup buttons per service (controller, Ollama, ChromaDB)
Log level filter: ToggleGroup for debug/info/warn/error
Text search: Input with real-time filtering
Auto-scroll with pause-on-scroll-up, resume Button at bottom
Log lines: monospace font, color-coded Badge per level, timestamp
Download logs Button exports visible logs as .txt
Uses shadcn/ui: ScrollArea, ToggleGroup, Input, Button, Badge

items.title: Build coding tools status dashboard page
items.description: Dashboard page showing installed coding CLIs and agents using shadcn/ui Cards with status Badges, workspace Table, and IDE connection info with copy-to-clipboard.
acceptance_criteria: Tool Cards: Claude Code, Aider, Continue, Tabby with version and Badge status
Config Badge: green (local provider), yellow (cloud), red (not configured)
Workspaces Table: mounted projects, indexed doc counts, last re-index time
Quick action Buttons: re-index workspace, reconfigure tool
IDE connection Card: API endpoint URL with copy-to-clipboard for Cursor/Copilot/Cody
External links to each tool's documentation
Uses shadcn/ui: Card, Badge, Table, Button, Tooltip

items.title: Build terminal UI (TUI) dashboard with Textual
items.description: Terminal-based dashboard using Python Textual framework. Provides the same system monitoring and management as the web dashboard but directly in the terminal — no browser needed. Essential for headless servers and SSH sessions.
acceptance_criteria: TUI launches via 'bmt_ai_os tui' command
System overview tab: service health, CPU/RAM/disk/temp gauges, tok/s
Models tab: list installed models, pull/remove with progress bar
Logs tab: live-tail with service filter and log level filter
Providers tab: list providers with status, switch active provider
RAG tab: query input with streaming response display
Keyboard navigation: tab switching, vim-style scrolling
Runs over SSH without X11 forwarding
Built with Python Textual (matches controller language)
Color theme matches web dashboard dark mode

items.title: Install PyTorch and ML training framework for ARM64
items.description: Package PyTorch (ARM64-native), Hugging Face Transformers, and core ML libraries into the OS image. Enable on-device model training and fine-tuning. PyTorch must use ARM NEON/SVE optimizations and CUDA when available on Jetson.
acceptance_criteria: PyTorch installed with ARM64 NEON/SVE optimizations (pip install torch)
Jetson target: PyTorch uses CUDA GPU acceleration
RK3588/Pi targets: PyTorch runs CPU-only with NEON vectorization
Hugging Face Transformers library installed
Hugging Face Datasets library installed
torch.cuda.is_available() returns True on Jetson, False on others
Basic training smoke test: fine-tune a 100-param model on dummy data

items.title: Implement LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning pipeline
items.description: Enable lightweight fine-tuning of small LLMs (1-3B params) on edge hardware using LoRA and QLoRA. Integrate PEFT library with Hugging Face Trainer. Target: fine-tune a 1.5B model on Jetson Orin with 8GB RAM.
acceptance_criteria: PEFT library installed with LoRA and QLoRA support
bitsandbytes installed for 4-bit quantization (QLoRA)
Fine-tune Qwen2.5-1.5B with LoRA on custom dataset (Jetson: <4GB VRAM)
Fine-tune with QLoRA on same model (<2GB VRAM for 4-bit base)
Training produces adapter weights saved to /var/lib/bmt_ai_os/adapters/
Adapter weights loadable by Ollama via Modelfile
Training script: bmt_ai_os train --model qwen2.5:1.5b --data /path/to/data
Training resumes from checkpoint on interruption

items.title: Build training data preparation tools
items.description: CLI tools for preparing training datasets from local documents, code, and conversations. Convert raw data into instruction-tuning format (Alpaca, ShareGPT, ChatML). Validate and preview datasets before training.
acceptance_criteria: bmt_ai_os data prepare --input /docs --format alpaca — convert docs to training format
Supported input: .txt, .md, .jsonl, .csv, code files
Output formats: Alpaca (instruction/input/output), ShareGPT (conversations), ChatML
Auto-generate instruction/response pairs from documents using local LLM
bmt_ai_os data validate /path/to/dataset.jsonl — check format, stats, issues
bmt_ai_os data preview /path/to/dataset.jsonl — show sample entries
Dataset splitting: auto train/eval split (default 90/10)

items.title: Add Jupyter Notebook server for interactive training
items.description: Pre-installed Jupyter Notebook/Lab accessible via browser for interactive model training, experimentation, and data exploration. Auto-configured with PyTorch and HF libraries.
acceptance_criteria: JupyterLab installed and launchable via bmt_ai_os jupyter
Served on port 8888, accessible from browser
Pre-configured kernel with PyTorch, Transformers, PEFT, Datasets
Example notebooks: LoRA fine-tuning, data preparation, model evaluation
Token authentication enabled by default
Auto-detected GPU/NPU shown in notebook environment info
Notebooks saved to /var/lib/bmt_ai_os/notebooks/

items.title: Implement training monitoring and TensorBoard integration
items.description: Training progress monitoring with loss curves, learning rate, and GPU/NPU utilization. TensorBoard for visualization. Integration with dashboard for training job status.
acceptance_criteria: TensorBoard installed, launchable via bmt_ai_os tensorboard
Served on port 6006, accessible from browser
Training logs written to /var/lib/bmt_ai_os/runs/ in TensorBoard format
Metrics tracked: loss, eval loss, learning rate, GPU memory, tok/s
Dashboard integration: training job Card on system overview page
Training job status: idle/running/complete/failed with progress %
Estimated time remaining based on current throughput

items.title: Build model export and deployment pipeline
items.description: Export fine-tuned models from training into formats deployable by the inference stack. Merge LoRA adapters into base model, convert to GGUF for llama.cpp, or create Ollama Modelfile for direct serving.
acceptance_criteria: bmt_ai_os model merge --base qwen2.5:1.5b --adapter /path/to/lora — merge LoRA weights
bmt_ai_os model export --format gguf — convert merged model to GGUF (Q4_K_M default)
bmt_ai_os model export --format ollama — create Ollama Modelfile and register model
Exported model immediately available in Ollama (ollama run my-fine-tuned-model)
Export preserves tokenizer and chat template
Validation: run inference on 10 test prompts, compare base vs fine-tuned

items.title: Add Apple Silicon board support package (Asahi Linux, CPU-only)
items.description: BSP for Apple Silicon Macs (M1-M4) via Asahi Linux. CPU-only — no GPU acceleration on Linux (Apple GPU driver incomplete). ARM64 CPU with NEON is exceptionally fast for LLM inference via llama.cpp. Highest single-core performance of any Tier 1 target. Unified memory enables large models that don't fit on other boards.
acceptance_criteria: BMT AI OS boots on Apple Silicon via Asahi Linux (M1/M2/M3/M4)
CPU-only inference via llama.cpp with ARM NEON optimization
Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 achieves 30-50 tok/s (CPU-only, M1 baseline)
16GB+ unified RAM enables 13B+ models (not possible on other Tier 1 boards)
Ollama runs natively, no GPU passthrough needed
containerd and Docker CLI functional on Asahi Linux
All coding CLIs and dashboard operational
Known limitation documented: no Metal/GPU acceleration on Linux
